name: Hill Up, Hill Down Trail
name_original: Cerro acima, Cerro abaixo — percurso circular (PR7)
description: Jog the 8.1-mile (13.0 km) "Hill up, Hill down — circular route (PR7)" with 973 feet (297 metres) of elevation gain, rated moderate.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Vaqueiros, a tranquil parish in the Algarve region of Portugal, traverse diverse landscapes characterized by rural scenery and gentle hills. The area features well-maintained trail networks, including paths that wind through almond groves and past intermittent watercourses. This setting provides a peaceful backdrop for running, offering glimpses into traditional rural life. Best jogging routes around Vaqueiros The most popular jogging route is Almond Tree Path (PR8), a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route is especially picturesque when almond trees are in bloom. Another top favourite among local runners is Hill Up, Hill Down Trail, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This route features varied terrain with ascents and descents across the landscape. Local runners also love the Canaviais do Barranco do Ribeirão — circular route (PR6), a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Vaqueiros is defined by its peaceful rural scenery, almond groves, and rolling hills. The network offers options for varied ability levels, with routes classified as moderate and difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Vaqueiros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Vaqueiros?
A: There are currently &lt;em&gt;over 7 running routes&lt;/em&gt; around Vaqueiros listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for joggers. These routes range in difficulty from moderate to difficult, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Vaqueiros?
A: The region is particularly picturesque between &lt;strong&gt;January and March&lt;/strong&gt;, when the almond trees are in bloom, transforming the landscape with beautiful white and pink hues. This makes for a stunning backdrop to your run. The mild climate of the Algarve generally makes it suitable for outdoor activities year-round, but spring offers unique scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Vaqueiros?
A: Yes, Vaqueiros offers several circular running routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418024611/canaviais-do-barranco-do-ribeirao-circular-route-pr6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canaviais do Barranco do Ribeirão — circular route (PR6)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 miles (7.3 km) long and takes you through peaceful rural landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418051633/almond-tree-path-pr8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Tree Path (PR8)&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a circular trail.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails around Vaqueiros?
A: Jogging routes around Vaqueiros are characterized by peaceful rural scenery, winding through almond groves and past intermittent watercourses. You'll experience diverse landscapes with gentle hills, offering glimpses into traditional rural life. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421575315/hill-up-hill-down-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill Up, Hill Down Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide varied terrain with ascents and descents across the landscape.

Q: Are there any moderate running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While most routes are classified as moderate to difficult, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418051633/almond-tree-path-pr8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Tree Path (PR8)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418024611/canaviais do barranco do ribeirao-circular-route-pr6" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canaviais do Barranco do Ribeirão — circular route (PR6)&lt;/a&gt; are both moderate and could be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run, or for families with some jogging experience. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your group's ability.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see while running in Vaqueiros?
A: Along your runs, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6382163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira do Rio Seco&lt;/a&gt;, an intermittent watercourse common in the Algarve, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6374185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing Ribeira do Beliche&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6374121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata Nacional das Terras da Ordem&lt;/a&gt; forest and ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/210220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anta da Masmorra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Vaqueiros dog-friendly?
A: Generally, the rural trails around Vaqueiros are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control and you adhere to local regulations regarding pets in natural areas. It's always a good idea to bring water for your dog, especially on longer routes or during warmer months, as specific water sources might be intermittent.

Q: How long are the typical running routes in Vaqueiros?
A: The running routes in Vaqueiros vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418051633/almond-tree-path-pr8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Tree Path (PR8)&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 6.9 miles (11.1 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422423711/in-search-of-the-enchanted-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;In Search of the Enchanted Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt; extends to about 8.6 miles (13.8 km). You'll find options ranging from around 4.5 miles (7.3 km) up to nearly 9 miles (14 km).

Q: What is the elevation gain like on Vaqueiros' running trails?
A: The trails in Vaqueiros feature rolling hills, so you can expect some elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421575315/hill-up-hill-down-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill Up, Hill Down Trail&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of approximately 297 meters (974 feet), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421775246/serra-de-alcaria-do-cume-circular-route-pr15-tvr" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Alcaria do Cume — circular route (PR15 TVR)&lt;/a&gt; has about 372 meters (1,220 feet) of ascent. This provides a good challenge and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the running routes?
A: Yes, the diverse landscape of Vaqueiros offers several scenic spots. You can find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Algarve Nature&lt;/a&gt; highlight, which provides expansive vistas of the surrounding natural beauty. Many routes traverse elevated areas, offering panoramic views of the rural countryside.

Q: What makes the running experience in Vaqueiros unique?
A: The running experience in Vaqueiros is unique due to its tranquil, bucolic atmosphere combined with diverse landscapes. It offers a chance to jog through traditional rural settings, past blooming almond groves in season, and along well-maintained trail networks that provide both challenge and serenity. It's a peaceful escape into nature.
tips: Vaqueiros is a small parish in the municipality of Alcoutim. It is a very peaceful place, where several hiking trails in the region begin. Like other villages in the Algarve, the population of Vaqueiros has reduced significantly over the years, further evidencing a bucolic and peaceful atmosphere.

The small Ribeira da Foupanilha is crossed several times along the PR7 route. Like other watercourses in the Algarve, its volume can be drastically different depending on the season. It is worth paying attention to the fact that, sometimes, in rainy seasons, crossing it can be a somewhat difficult task.

The Cova dos Mouros Mining Park, located in Alcoutim, Portugal, is a theme park and archaeological site. It offers a thematic route that portrays the history of mining and metallurgy from the Chalcolithic period to the beginning of the 20th century. Furthermore, it serves as a reserve for animal species such as ostriches and donkeys, and is located on the former premises of the Cova dos Mouros Mine.

faqs.question: How challenging is this route for jogging?
faqs.answer: The "Hill up, Hill down — circular route (PR7)" is rated as moderate for jogging. It features significant elevation changes, with approximately 297 meters of ascent and 301 meters of descent over its 13 km distance. While the overall duration for jogging is around 1 hour and 40 minutes, the varied terrain and elevation make it a good workout. For very steep sections, power hiking (fast walking) can be more efficient than running.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, typical of a PR (pequena rota) trail. While specific details for this exact PR7 are limited, similar trails often include dirt roads and single paths. Given the name "Hill up, Hill down," be prepared for uneven surfaces and significant gradients. Good footgear is recommended to handle the changes in elevation and potential technical sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several interesting locations. You'll encounter the settlement of Vaqueiros, the scenic Ribeira da Foupanilha, and the historical Cova dos Mouros Mining Park. These highlights offer diverse scenery and a glimpse into the local area.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with other well-known paths. It fully covers the "Cerro acima, Cerro abaixo" trail and also passes through sections of the "Via Algarviana - Connection 8: Vaqueiros - Martim Longo" (45% coverage), "Em Busca do Vale Encantado" (8% coverage), and the main "Via Algarviana" (8% coverage).

faqs.question: What's the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal advice for this PR7 isn't available, generally for trails with significant elevation, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is often at its best. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not explicitly state whether this specific PR7 is dog-friendly. However, many PR trails in Portugal allow dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required for the "Hill up, Hill down — circular route (PR7)". PR trails are typically publicly accessible without special permits, but it's always good practice to look for any local signage at the trailhead.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact PR7 are not provided. However, for circular routes like this, parking is usually available near the starting point, often in or near a local village or designated trailhead area. Given that the route passes through Vaqueiros, there might be parking options there.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Hydration is crucial, so carry enough water. Depending on the weather, layers of clothing are advisable. For downhill sections, which can be hard on joints, consider bringing trekking poles for stability and to reduce impact. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the settlement of Vaqueiros, where you might find basic facilities or opportunities to refill water. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire 13 km duration, as dedicated water sources or facilities directly on the trail are not guaranteed.

faqs.question: What's the best technique for handling the 'hill up, hill down' sections?
faqs.answer: For uphill sections, take short, quick steps, lean slightly forward, and maintain an upright posture for optimal breathing. Don't be afraid to power hike if it's very steep. For downhills, focus on short, quick steps, landing lightly on your midfoot, and looking ahead to anticipate obstacles. Avoid leaning back too much, as this can strain your knees. Confidence and scanning the path are key for efficient and safe descents.
